prompt:
Support-analytics request. Our weekly Jira export broke in the worst way: the script still hits GET /rest/api/3/search with startAt/total, and since Atlassian removed that interface it silently returns nothing, so leadership got an empty report and nobody noticed for two sprints. I also found out the intern's "fix" stops paging the moment a page comes back with zero issues, which the new search API apparently does mid-scan sometimes. Rebuild this properly as a small Python package jiraexport/ (stdlib only, python3, no pip installs). test_jql_export.py is the acceptance contract.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Atlassian documentation — at minimum the v3 issue-search reference for the enhanced JQL search endpoint (request shape, what the default fields selection is, maxResults semantics, how continuation actually works and what the response carries on the last page), plus the search-and-reconcile guide (consistency model) and the basic-auth page. Only first-party Atlassian pages (developer.atlassian.com and Atlassian-hosted API specs) count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live official docs against them — if anything disagrees, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests import: JiraSearchClient and JiraApiError from jiraexport.client, and export_jsonl from jiraexport.exporter.

- JiraSearchClient(base_url, email, api_token) authenticates the way the basic-auth page says scripts should. search_all(jql, fields=..., page_size=..., expand=None) is a generator/iterable of issues that drives the documented search endpoint with the documented request body: the JQL, the page size (find the documented default and use it when the caller doesn't say), the explicit field list (find out what you get if you don't send one — that's why it's mandatory here), and expand only when given, in the type the docs say it is (it's not a list). Never touch the removed legacy search path and never send its pagination fields — the mock answers 410 to anything but the current endpoint.
- Continuation must follow the response's token contract exactly: nothing on the first request, then the token from each response verbatim, stopping only when the response says it's done. A page with zero issues but a token is NOT done — that cost us a report once already. Yield issues in arrival order with their id/key/fields intact: whatever JSON types Jira sent (ints, floats, null, nested status/resolution objects, arrays, unicode) must come through untouched.
- export_jsonl(client, jql, fields, path, page_size=...) streams the search into a JSONL file, one {id, key, fields} object per line, UTF-8, and returns the row count. Reading the file back must reproduce the exact typed values.
- HTTP failures raise JiraApiError exposing status and error_messages decoded from Jira's error collection, with a readable message. The token, the email, and the encoded auth blob must never appear in error text.

Acceptance: python3 test_jql_export.py — it runs a loopback http.server mock, no real Jira site or credentials anywhere. test_jql_export.py and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Jira Cloud platform REST API v3 enhanced JQL search (POST /rest/api/3/search/jql)

official_sources.title: The Jira Cloud platform REST API — official OpenAPI v3 specification
official_sources.used_for: GET/POST /rest/api/3/search/jql operations ('Search for issues using JQL enhanced search'); SearchAndReconcileRequestBean body fields jql, nextPageToken, maxResults (default 50), fields (default id — 'By default, this resource returns IDs only'), expand ('defined as a comma-delimited string of values'), properties, fieldsByKeys, reconcileIssues; nextPageToken parameter doc: 'The first page has a nextPageToken of null... The nextPageToken field is not included in the response for the last page'; SearchAndReconcileResults {issues: [IssueBean], isLast, nextPageToken ('If this result represents the last or the only page this token will be null. This token will expire in 7 days'), names, schema}; maxResults doc: 'API may return fewer items per page where a large number of fields or properties are requested'; JQL must be bounded; 400 'Returned if the search request is invalid' with ErrorCollection {errorMessages, errors}

official_sources.title: Search and reconcile — Atlassian Developer
official_sources.used_for: the enhanced-search consistency model behind /rest/api/{2|3}/search/jql: 'The API doesn't provide read-after-write consistency by default'; reconcileIssues accepts a maximum of 50 issue IDs for read-after-write consistency; nextPageToken is the response's continuation field

official_sources.title: Basic auth for REST APIs — Atlassian Developer
official_sources.used_for: Authorization: Basic base64(email:api_token) — the Atlassian account email joined with an API token by a colon, base64-encoded; API tokens instead of passwords

research.language_rationale: The reporting stack consuming this export is Python, and the catalog item targets the current enhanced-search wire contract — the nextPageToken continuation (including token-bearing empty pages), the explicit fields selection whose default is id-only, and Jira's JSON-typed field values — which the community jira PyPI package still wraps around the removed startAt/total model. Python's stdlib urllib/json/http.server covers the documented POST surface hermetically with zero dependencies.
